ddls:
CREATE TABLE "countries" (
  "country_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each country */,
  "country_name" TEXT /* Name of the country */,
  "region_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the region */,
  "population" INTEGER /* Total population of the country */,
  "capital" TEXT /* Capital city of the country */,
  "official_language" TEXT /* Official language of the country */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("country_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_countries_region_id FOREIGN KEY ("region_id") REFERENCES regions ("region_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "regions" (
  "region_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each region */,
  "region_name" TEXT /* Name of the geographic region */,
  "continent" TEXT /* Continent where the region belongs */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("region_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "covid_statistics" (
  "stat_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for the statistics entry */,
  "country_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the country */,
  "date" TEXT /* Date of the statistics entry */,
  "confirmed" INTEGER /* Total confirmed cases */,
  "deaths" INTEGER /* Total deaths */,
  "recovered" INTEGER /* Total recovered cases */,
  "active" INTEGER /* Total active cases */,
  "new_cases" INTEGER /* Newly confirmed cases since last report */,
  "new_deaths" INTEGER /* Newly reported deaths since last report */,
  "new_recovered" INTEGER /* Newly recovered cases since last report */,
  "source" TEXT /* Source of the data (e.g., WHO, national health department) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("stat_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_covid_statistics_country_id FOREIGN KEY ("country_id") REFERENCES countries ("country_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "death_rates" (
  "rate_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for the death rate entry */,
  "country_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the country */,
  "date" TEXT /* Date of the death rate calculation */,
  "deaths_percentage" REAL /* Percentage of deaths relative to confirmed cases */,
  "deaths_per_100k" REAL /* Deaths per 100,000 population */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("rate_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_death_rates_country_id FOREIGN KEY ("country_id") REFERENCES countries ("country_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "recovery_rates" (
  "rate_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for the recovery rate entry */,
  "country_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the country */,
  "date" TEXT /* Date of the recovery rate calculation */,
  "recovered_percentage" REAL /* Percentage of recoveries relative to confirmed cases */,
  "recovery_rate" REAL /* Recovery rate (cases per 100,000 population) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("rate_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_recovery_rates_country_id FOREIGN KEY ("country_id") REFERENCES countries ("country_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "region_statistics" (
  "region_stat_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each regional statistics entry */,
  "region_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the region */,
  "date" TEXT /* Date of the regional statistics entry */,
  "total_confirmed" INTEGER /* Total confirmed cases in the region */,
  "total_deaths" INTEGER /* Total deaths in the region */,
  "total_recovered" INTEGER /* Total recovered cases in the region */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("region_stat_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_region_statistics_region_id FOREIGN KEY ("region_id") REFERENCES regions ("region_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., researcher, public health official) */,
  "password" TEXT /* User password */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Timestamp of last login */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the data */,
  "access_time" TEXT /* Timestamp of the access */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, edit) */,
  "data_accessed" TEXT /* Specific data accessed (e.g., country statistics, region statistics) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "statistics_history" (
  "history_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for historical entries */,
  "country_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the country */,
  "stat_date" TEXT /* Date of the statistics entry */,
  "confirmed" INTEGER /* Total confirmed cases on that date */,
  "deaths" INTEGER /* Total deaths on that date */,
  "recovered" INTEGER /* Total recovered cases on that date */,
  "source" TEXT /* Source of the data (e.g., WHO, national health department) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("history_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_statistics_history_country_id FOREIGN KEY ("country_id") REFERENCES countries ("country_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "vaccinations" (
  "vaccine_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each vaccination entry */,
  "country_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the country */,
  "date" TEXT /* Date of the vaccination entry */,
  "total_vaccinated" INTEGER /* Total number of people vaccinated */,
  "fully_vaccinated" INTEGER /* Total number of people fully vaccinated */,
  "vaccine_type" TEXT /* Type of vaccine used (e.g., Pfizer, Moderna) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("vaccine_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_vaccinations_country_id FOREIGN KEY ("country_id") REFERENCES countries ("country_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "variant_tracker" (
  "variant_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each variant entry */,
  "country_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the country */,
  "date" TEXT /* Date of the variant entry */,
  "variant_name" TEXT /* Name of the COVID-19 variant (e.g., Alpha, Delta) */,
  "cases" INTEGER /* Number of cases of the variant */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("variant_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_variant_tracker_country_id FOREIGN KEY ("country_id") REFERENCES countries ("country_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "public_health_measures" (
  "measure_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each public health measure entry */,
  "country_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the country */,
  "date" TEXT /* Date of the public health measure entry */,
  "measure_type" TEXT /* Type of public health measure (e.g., lockdown, mask mandate) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Detailed description of the public health measure */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("measure_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_public_health_measures_country_id FOREIGN KEY ("country_id") REFERENCES countries ("country_id")
)
